preamble: Spirantization of the voiced aspirated velar stop [ɡʱ] into the voiced velar fricative [ɣ] is a common evolutionary trajectory for velar stops, especially in Indo-European developments in Germanic and Greek.

examples.note: γ (gamma) transitioned from a voiced stop [ɡ] to a voiced fricative [ɣ].
PIE *ɡʱ evolved into Proto-Germanic *ɣ in medial positions.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
